narrative: Dear [Student's Name], imagine the admissions team reviewing your application like piecing together the story of your journey toward becoming a lawyer, and unfortunately, the picture that emerged pointed to some real hurdles in passing the bar exam. Your Year 3 grades placed you in the bottom decile of your school, which stood out as the strongest signal of concern—it suggested the rigors of advanced legal studies might have been an uphill battle, much like they've been for others in similar spots who struggled later on. This was echoed by your Year 1 decile, also at the bottom, painting a consistent early picture of academic challenges in a demanding environment. Your LSAT score of 35 added to that narrative, falling short of the benchmarks that typically light the path to bar success for applicants like you. With a middle family income bracket, the model saw fewer resources to cushion those academic pressures compared to peers with more support. Your undergraduate GPA of 3.3, while solid, didn't quite counterbalance these trends in the eyes of the prediction. Even factors like being a white female pursuing full-time studies nudged the story toward caution, as patterns from past students in those groups with your profile have shown higher risks. Together, these elements built a 58.5% estimated chance of bar failure, leading to the tough call to reject your application—not because we doubt your potential, but to protect you from investing time and heart into a path stacked against you right now. You've got strengths worth building on, like that UGPA, and with targeted steps to boost your academic standing, a brighter story could unfold next time. We're rooting for you to rewrite this chapter.
